verses.text: Rehoboam goes to Shechem, where Israel, led by Jeroboam, asks him to lighten the heavy yoke his father laid on them. He bids them return in three days. A nation's loyalty hangs on his answer. Wise rulers weigh the people's burdens before demanding their service.

Rehoboam goes to Shechem, where Israel, led by Jeroboam, asks him to lighten the heavy yoke his father laid on them. He bids them return in three days. A nation's loyalty hangs on his answer. Wise rulers weigh the people's burdens before demanding their service.

The old men counsel Rehoboam to speak kindly and serve the people, and they will be his servants forever. Gentleness wins hearts that force never could. A soft answer and a servant's spirit bind a people more surely than any show of power.

The old men counsel Rehoboam to speak kindly and serve the people, and they will be his servants forever. Gentleness wins hearts that force never could. A soft answer and a servant's spirit bind a people more surely than any show of power.

But Rehoboam forsakes the elders' counsel for the young men's, who urge him to threaten heavier burdens: 'I will chastise you with scorpions.' Pride despises wisdom. The folly that loves to flex its strength is the surest way to lose it.

But Rehoboam forsakes the elders' counsel for the young men's, who urge him to threaten heavier burdens: 'I will chastise you with scorpions.' Pride despises wisdom. The folly that loves to flex its strength is the surest way to lose it.

Rehoboam answers the people roughly, as the young men advised, refusing to lighten their load. Harshness hardens the breach. A leader who will not bend to mercy soon finds the people will not bend to him.

Rehoboam answers the people roughly, as the young men advised, refusing to lighten their load. Harshness hardens the breach. A leader who will not bend to mercy soon finds the people will not bend to him.

Rehoboam answers the people roughly, as the young men advised, refusing to lighten their load. Harshness hardens the breach. A leader who will not bend to mercy soon finds the people will not bend to him.

The king would not listen, for the turn of events was from God to fulfil His word; so Israel cried, 'What portion have we in David?' and went to their tents. Human folly serves God's purpose. He overrules even a king's pride to keep His sovereign word.

The king would not listen, for the turn of events was from God to fulfil His word; so Israel cried, 'What portion have we in David?' and went to their tents. Human folly serves God's purpose. He overrules even a king's pride to keep His sovereign word.

Rehoboam reigns only over Judah; when he sends Hadoram to collect tribute, Israel stones him, and the ten tribes rebel from David's house 'unto this day.' The kingdom is torn in two. Sin's harvest is division, and a breach lightly made is not lightly healed.
